q1_sink_dangerous.evidence: Sink is HttpResponse construction, which only builds an in-memory response object without network I/O or side effects.

q2_observable_after_return.evidence: The unescaped user input is embedded in the body of the returned HttpResponse object, so the XSS violation is present in the return value.

q3_result_in_memory.evidence: returns.kind is 'value' and the returned HttpResponse object is directly accessible in memory for inspection of its content/body.
